factors.summary: Debt leverage has shown improvement, as evidenced by a decrease in the net debt to net capitalization ratio and a reduction in commercial paper. These developments indicate progress in managing debt levels.

evidence.text: Our net debt to net capitalization ratio decreased to 36.2% at September 30, 2015 from 38.8% at December 31, 2014 . The decrease in this ratio was driven primarily by the reduction in our net capitalization of $460.3 million due to lower commercial paper during the current year.
evidence.subsection: Net Debt to Net Capitalization

evidence.text: Commercial paper and notes payable, net decreased in the 2015 period by $316.8 million.
evidence.subsection: Financing Activities

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in debt leverage metrics shows progress in debt management.

factors.summary: The backlog orders are decreasing across multiple segments, with significant declines observed in both the Energy and Refrigeration & Food Equipment segments. The Energy segment saw a 32.7% year-over-year decrease in backlog, while the Refrigeration & Food Equipment segment experienced an 18.3% decline.

evidence.text: Backlog was 156,631 compared to 232,739 in the prior year, a decrease of 32.7%. Bookings decreased by 33.2% in the third quarter and 24.9% year-to-date.
evidence.subsection: Energy

evidence.text: Backlog decreased $53.4 million in 2015, or 3.7%, to $1.4 billion... Refrigeration & Food Equipment backlog decreased 18.3%.
evidence.subsection: Operating Activities

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The backlog orders are decreasing significantly across multiple segments, indicating a decline in demand.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has shown improvement, with a notable increase in both the amount generated and its percentage relative to key metrics such as revenue and net earnings. The evidence suggests a positive trend, supported by higher cash inflows from operating activities and working capital, despite a stable overall context as mentioned in the introduction.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015 , we generated free cash flow of $521.2 million , representing 9.9% of revenue and 113.5% of net earnings from continuing operations. Free cash flow in 2015 increased $124.6 million over the prior year primarily due to higher cash inflow from working capital.
evidence.subsection: Free Cash Flow

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities increased approximately $127.1 million compared to the prior year period.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

evidence.text: Free cash flow is operating cash flow less capital spending, while adjusted working capital refers to accounts receivable, plus inventory, less accounts payable.
evidence.subsection: Operating Activities

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has improved, showing higher generation and better margins relative to revenue and net earnings.
